title: Add system test for shell completion
body:
There exists a unit test to ensure that shell completion functions are
defined. However there was no check about the quality of the provided
shell completions. Lets change that.

The idea is to create a general test that makes sure we are suggesting
containers,pods,images... for the correct commands. This works by
reading the command use line and checking for each arg if we provide
the correct suggestions for this arg.

It includes the following tests:
- flag suggestions if [options] is set
- container, pod, image, network, volume, registry completion
- path completion for the appropriate arg KEYWORDS (`PATH`,`CONTEXT`,etc.)
- no completion if there are no args
- completion for more than one arg if it ends with `...]`

The test does not cover completion values for flags and not every arg KEYWORD
is supported. This is still a huge improvement and covers most use cases.

This test spotted several inconsistencies between the completion and the
command use line. All of them have been adjusted to make the test pass.

The biggest advantage is that the completions always match the latest
command changes. So if someone changes the arguments for a command this
ensures that the completions must be adjusted.


<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@Luap99  LGTM, once you get past linter

@Luap99 I have been, all day, while juggling a bunch of other priorities. I hope to have some feedback by EOD, oops, it's EOD already. I hope to have some feedback within the next hour, at which time I have a hard cutoff. 

Really nice work as always. My feedback is so far very minor.
@edsantiago No problem, take your time. I just added support `podman-remote` with the force-push.

My day has run long. I realize this is rude, but may I just offer this diff instead of one-by-one comments? Basically: typo fixes, a suggestion for your EOL check, a fix for an improper `untag`, and cleanup of the `pause` image:

[8635-suggestions.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/5662239/8635-suggestions.txt)

This is just a first pass over the course of a long day; I'd like to make another pass tomorrow. 

One question: do you think it would be possible/easy to just run a subset of completion tests by default? This takes 37 seconds on my laptop, meaning it will take over a minute in CI. That's not a huge problem, but it's enough to make one consider.

Thanks as always. This is really nicely done.

> One question: do you think it would be possible/easy to just run a subset of completion tests by default? This takes 37 seconds on my laptop, meaning it will take over a minute in CI. That's not a huge problem, but it's enough to make one consider.

I would say the full test should run on every PR but it would be enough to only run this on one distro as root but for both `podman` and `podman-remote`. I don't want anyone breaking my completion logic.

Meetings for the next two hours; I need to shift gears now. 

All my comments are relatively minor, but I think they add up to where I'd like to see a new push. Again, this is really really nice work, thank you.

>I  would say the full test should run on every PR but it would be enough to only run this on one distro as root but for both podman and podman-remote

Understood. We have no mechanism for doing that, so we'll just have to suffer on all runs.

> > I  would say the full test should run on every PR but it would be enough to only run this on one distro as root but for both podman and podman-remote
> 
> Understood. We have no mechanism for doing that, so we'll just have to suffer on all runs.

@edsantiago I could add `skip_if_rootless` and `skip_if_no_selinux` that should work, no?

Yes, and that's actually pretty clever... but I'm having a cringe reaction to it, because it's not technically honest: it would need to be documented with a nudge-and-wink, "we're just skipping this to save CI cycles". And since the `sys` jobs run in parallel, it doesn't save any end-user time anyway. 

So let's just leave it. I was hoping I could think of a way to separate this and the `help` test into a separate run, a one-arch-only earlier one (that would run under CI-colon-DOCS also); but I can't think of a way, and premature optimization etc etc. Thanks for giving it your consideration.
Ok, I added your requested changes. This should be this ready now.

/lgtm
/hold

Thanks again, and thanks for your patience with me
